commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Needs contributor-owned real behavior proof and normal maintainer UI review; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue.; Cleared: The diff is limited to Control UI rendering, CSS, and tests, with no dependency, CI, secret, package, or code-execution surface changes. (sha=84cbc522625fca65d3512e011194489cc8cf28b2)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the Dreaming Imported Insights cluster selector into a labeled, count-rich tab control with ARIA semantics, keyboard navigation, CSS, and focused view tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main renders Imported Insights clusters with compact label-only day-chip controls and counts only in the selected panel. I did not run a live browser reproduction.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor-owned real behavior proof and normal maintainer UI review; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue.

target.title: fix(ui): clarify imported insights cluster tabs
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #80301 by turning the Imported Insights cluster chips into a labeled, more prominent horizontal tab bar.
- Adds the cluster counts directly to each tab so users can scan chats, sensitive clusters, and candidate signals before switching.
- Adds ARIA tab semantics with roving focus and keyboard navigation, plus focused Dreaming view regressions.

## Problem summary

Dreaming -> Diary -> Imported Insights already exposed cluster navigation, but the controls were small label-only chips. The useful counts were only visible after selecting a cluster, which made the row easy to miss and hard to scan in large imports.

## Root cause

`renderDiaryImportsSection` reused the generic diary day-chip style and rendered only `cluster.label` in each control. The memory-wiki import insight payload already carries the relevant counts, but the view did not surface them in the navigation row or provide stronger selected/focus semantics for this cluster-switching control.

## Architectural reasoning

This stays entirely in the Control UI presentation layer. The memory-wiki plugin remains the source of cluster data and ordering, the Dreaming controller contract is unchanged, and the view only formats the existing `WikiImportInsightCluster` fields. The patch does not alter runtime behavior outside the Dreaming UI, gateway/session flows, concurrency, cleanup logic, API behavior, or plugin contracts. Sort controls are intentionally not included because the issue discussion calls the exact sort dimensions a product/UX choice; preserving backend order keeps this PR narrow and reviewable.

## Testing performed

- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm format:check -- ui/src/ui/views/dreaming.ts ui/src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts ui/src/styles/dreams.css`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD`
- `pnpm lint:core -- --threads=1`
- `.\node_modules\.bin\tsgo.cmd -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `.\node_modules\.bin\tsgo.cmd -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-ui.tsbuildinfo`
- `pnpm ui:build`
- `codex review -c model="gpt-5.4-mini" --base upstream/main --title "fix(ui): clarify imported insights cluster tabs"` found missing tab keyboard support; that finding was fixed and covered by a new keyboard navigation test. A follow-up review rerun timed out locally without returning findings, so the final diff was also manually reviewed after the full local proof above.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Imported Insights cluster navigation is now visually prominent, labeled as `Filter by cluster`, shows useful counts in each control, and exposes correct tab selection/focus semantics.

Real environment tested: Local Windows checkout on branch `Hemant/improve-imported-insights-tabs`, rebased onto `fb53c2d610924e78b308765ece0dc4f1c7f8002a`, Node `v22.15.0` with pnpm running under the repository install.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the Dreaming view Vitest file after the final rebase with `pnpm test ui/src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`, then ran formatting, changed checks, lint, direct tsgo core/UI-test configs, diff whitespace checks, and `pnpm ui:build`.

Evidence after fix: The Dreaming view test now asserts the Imported Insights cluster tab label, count-rich tab text, `role="tablist"`/`role="tab"`, `aria-selected`, `aria-controls`, roving `tabIndex`, `role="tabpanel"`, and ArrowRight keyboard navigation from Travel to Health.

Observed result after fix: The focused Dreaming view test passed with 16 tests, including the new keyboard navigation regression. Formatting, changed checks, lint, direct tsgo checks, whitespace checks, and the Control UI Vite production build all passed after rebasing onto latest upstream.

What was not tested: I did not run a live localhost Control UI session with a real imported ChatGPT dataset or capture screenshots. The UI behavior was verified through the existing jsdom view test harness and the production Control UI build.

## Risk analysis

- Runtime behavior: Limited to Control UI rendering for Dreaming -> Diary -> Imported Insights.
- Concurrency/session/gateway flows: No changes.
- Cleanup/lifecycle handling: No changes.
- API behavior: No public API, gateway protocol, plugin manifest, or controller payload changes.
- Tests: Adds focused coverage for the new tab label/count semantics and keyboard navigation.
- Backward compatibility: Existing memory-wiki data contracts and backend cluster ordering are preserved.
- Developer ergonomics: The count formatter is typed against `WikiImportInsightCluster` and keeps the display logic local to the view.

## Backward compatibility

Backward compatible. Existing Imported Insights data renders through the same `wikiImportInsights.clusters` payload; this only changes presentation and accessibility semantics for the cluster selector.
target.author: hemantsudarshan
